summaries.68014: Comments note that iOS 16 introduced a system alert whenever an app accesses clipboard data, making type-specific APIs (hasImages, hasURLs) critical to avoid prompting users unnecessarily. A third-party package `uipasteboard` was published as a workaround. A contributor offered to submit a PR if given direction.
summaries.170721: Reproduced on stable 3.32.4 and master 3.33 on macOS; Windows not verified. Root cause identified: the desktop accessibility bridge does not propagate the obscureText flag to the embedding. The fix requires work in the desktop embedding layer; no PR yet.
summaries.80109: Comments confirm web and desktop already fully obscure all characters (implemented in PR #56794), while mobile shows the last typed character. The discrepancy is intentional per platform norms. Issue #65352 tracks honoring the Android system setting.
summaries.90355: A commenter describes an accessibility use case: a WidgetSpan containing a math image is scaled down instead of being scrollable. A concrete test case was provided showing the WidgetSpan content is clipped and does not scroll horizontally when the TextField is narrower than the span.
summaries.45804: Confirmed still reproducible on iOS in Flutter 3.27.1; Android works correctly. Root cause in comments: on iOS, the selection baseOffset is reduced before the character is actually deleted, firing the listener twice. Workaround: set `enableInteractiveSelection: false`.
summaries.183078: Reproduction confirmed on Flutter 3.41.2, macOS 26.3, Chrome with Korean 2-Set IME. A community contributor expressed interest in fixing it as they use a Korean Flutter Web app in production. Routed to team-web.
summaries.88135: Comments note that non-collapsed selections on macOS do not visually require affinity (rendered as highlighted rectangles). iPad with hardware keyboard was not tested. A suggestion was made to document explicit affinity assumptions when not collapsed.
summaries.150522: Comments confirm this is a bug (inconsistent state) related to #34749 and PR #148984. Workaround: disable shake-to-edit via `UIApplication.shared.applicationSupportsShakeToEdit = false` in AppDelegate.swift. The bug also triggers with any native dialog (e.g., file picker) taking focus.
summaries.83733: Discussion converged on naming: `OsSingleActivator` or `AdaptiveSingleActivator` with fields `osControl`/`osMeta`. Electron's CommandOrControl activator was cited as inspiration. Flutter's existing default text editing shortcuts maintain separate maps per platform.
summaries.90710: Comments clarify the request is for desktop-style keyboard date/time entry (inline with validation on input), not a picker dialog. The team acknowledged the idea is reasonable. No PR or concrete plan.
summaries.117139: Triage confirmed reproduction on master 3.7 on Android and iOS. Root cause identified: Skia SkParagraph's `getRectsForRange` API; a Skia bug was filed at https://bugs.chromium.org/p/skia/issues/detail?id=14035. A whitespace workaround was suggested.
summaries.165389: Comments clarify SelectionArea only supports text. Image selection requires first solving rich content copy/paste (#104206). A SelectableRegion adapter workaround exists but only copies plain-text representations.
summaries.127694: Engine PR #42268 (landed in Flutter 3.13) partially resolved autofill timing; Bitwarden can now autofill via keyboard shortcut, context menu, and extension when a field is focused. Autofill via extension click while no field is focused still fails.
summaries.142941: One triage comment notes Chrome appears to auto-suggest passwords for empty-typed inputs in form tags with `aria-autocomplete="list"`. Further investigation was noted as needed; no fix landed.
summaries.124784: Comments argue the `keyboard_actions` package workaround produces poor UX in Cupertino apps. A request was added to also expose iOS `inputView` (not just `inputAccessoryView`). The issue was reopened after initially being closed.
summaries.176055: One comment notes the Android 17 feature has landed and Flutter can begin adapting it, referencing internal bug b/444503345.
summaries.164472: A reproduction demonstrating all 107+ form fields rebuilding on each `didChange` call was provided with screen recordings showing the proposed fix. The issue remains open awaiting team input.
summaries.96021: Comments confirm the root cause: the simulator converts all keys to lowercase per a TODO in the code. The intention was to deprecate old simulation code once the new KeyEvent-based system was ready.
summaries.117140: Triage confirmed reproduction on master 3.7. A comment notes this is possibly related to issue #117139. No root cause or fix proposed.
summaries.89394: Comment from @Renzo-Olivares clarifies delta-based editing should retain `oldText` so mismatched states can be detected and discarded. Framework-to-engine updates still overwrite the full value, so this is not a current blocker.
summaries.150815: Comments suggest this may be related to #137666 and #150813. The hypothesis is that after hot restart the FlutterTextInputView is not properly removed, leaving the autocorrect highlight on screen. No fix or PR.
summaries.140764: Only routing and cc comments; no substantive new information.
summaries.87064: Comments confirm ctrl+T on Windows/Linux does nothing. `shift+ctrl+A` and `shift+ctrl+E` (extend selection to line start/end on macOS) were reported as also not working, with a screencast reproduction.
summaries.151980: Triage confirmed reproduction on Android with TalkBack on stable 3.22.3 and master 3.24. A workaround using `Semantics(textField: true)` around the suffixIcon was posted. Similar to previously fixed issue #39918.
summaries.45841: Comments broaden scope: the lack of `onBlur` affects mobile too when users navigate between fields without using Done. The FocusNode listener workaround is acknowledged as functional. No PR.
summaries.91731: Comments suggest adding a codelab showing how to implement a TextInputFormatter mask. The documentation was noted as insufficient; `FilteringTextInputFormatter` is now too complex to serve as a reference example.
summaries.76632: Comments clarify convenience statics for shift-toggle characters (like `ampersand`) are missing but the underlying mechanism works via `LogicalKeyboardKey(0x26)`. Issue #77048 was opened separately for layout-dependent key mapping problems.
summaries.181682: Comments from the text input team note two drawbacks to `CupertinoSelectableText`: it would not adapt to Android handles/menus, and the long-term goal is to make `SelectableRegion` the universal solution. Moving Cupertino SelectableText tests to `test/material/cupertino/` was agreed as a short-term fix.
summaries.37390: Comments suspect the formatter's result overwrites not-yet-processed inputs buffered by the engine. A commenter reports the same issue with Zebra enterprise keyboards. Triage was unable to reproduce on latest master because the slow formatter caused the app to crash.
summaries.61195: Comments note the Material spec gives no guidance on scrollable hint text. No reference implementation exists for horizontally scrollable hints; feasibility is in question. No PR.
summaries.74347: Comments identify the issue was caused by PR #72803 which was already reverted. The whole feature was potentially going to be rewritten, so the issue was kept open as a test case.
summaries.100416: Discussion clarifies the requested "clipboard" option is a clipboard history list, not standard copy/paste. On Android 12 testing, no such native toolbar option was found. A contextual menus design doc was shared.
summaries.21193: Confirmed reproduced on stable 1.20.3 and master 3.5. Additional scenario: after backgrounding and foregrounding the app, the TextField still appears focused with cursor but keyboard is gone, requiring a tap to reopen. A regression where `EditableText` cannot recapture keyboard focus was also noted.
summaries.168731: Confirmed the behavior change (requiring two Enter presses to submit after `TextInputAction.next`) occurs on all platforms: Android, iOS, web, and desktop.
summaries.132272: Comments discuss that macOS handles the accent panel via the OS; dismissal may be achievable via InputMethodKit's `hidePalettes`. Using `DeleteCharacterIntent` instead of `onKey` was suggested but noted not to work.
summaries.176753: Comments confirm the UX issue with video. Issue #175090 was noted as related. A commenter argues `ignore` should not be deprecated since typing without selecting is a valid pattern.
summaries.114950: Subtext gap PR #145087 filed but reverted due to internal test failure. Label position investigated in PR #121753. Error text color fixed in PR #119225 (merged). Suffix icon alignment added to tracking. Cursor width/height/color and layout bugs remain unresolved.
summaries.140751: Comments clarify the proposal: cross-field validation error messages should appear at form level, not under a specific field. No existing API supports this.
summaries.184597: Comments reveal the current behavior (8 dots for family emoji) was previously intentional. After offline discussion, the team agreed Flutter should show a single obscured character per extended grapheme cluster. Chromium made a similar change.
summaries.99436: A comment from @jonahwilliams identifies the root cause: `textCapitalization` is used when creating the native text field; it may not be possible to update it without tearing down and recreating the field. Both Android and iOS engine references were provided.
summaries.102101: Discussion surfaces ideas including writing a custom IME with a testing interface, testing composing across focus switches, backspace/Escape behavior, and candidate menu navigation. @LongCatIsLooong suggested a custom IME approach since third-party IMEs like Samsung Korean behave unpredictably.
summaries.72625: Only a stale comment asking for updates; no substantive new information.
summaries.64272: Comments identify the crash likely occurs during navigation when the widget tree is being disposed while a key event arrives. A fix was added via PR #64474 as a temporary workaround pending further analysis.
summaries.163244: Comments favor making `_CaretPainter` public and wiring it via `InputDecorationTheme` or `TextSelectionThemeData`, similar to `InteractiveInkFeatureFactory`. A contributor offered to implement once the approach is decided.
summaries.104547: Comments note all mentioned blocking issues have been fixed. However, programmatic multi-line selection with `selectable: true` is still not possible via `SelectionArea`.
summaries.10036: Comments discuss API compatibility concerns and the general awkwardness of the `builder` pattern in `FormField`. The conclusion was that an API change is not happening but the issue remains open for study.
summaries.171732: Comments discuss whether `SystemContextMenu` should be exposed outside editable text, noting it conflicts with Flutter's principle of owning the UI. A triage note acknowledges it is not feasible to completely eliminate the Flutter-drawn context menu for platforms without a built-in one.
summaries.127831: Comments confirm the feature was implemented: merged via https://github.com/flutter/engine/pull/50293 and available in Flutter 3.22.0 as `TextInputType.visiblePassword`.
summaries.183227: A community contributor volunteered to implement the proposal. Triage noted this involves a public API change to `TextSelectionThemeData`, requiring team review before any PR.
summaries.39971: Comments confirm that Android and iOS do not expose frame-by-frame keyboard animation data. The suggestion was to fake the animation similar to how dialogs are animated around keyboard changes.
summaries.177801: Only a cc to @Renzo-Olivares and @LongCatIsLooong; no substantive new information.
summaries.108668: Team welcomes a PR; existing mobile text selection handle code could likely be reused for desktop touch screens. A contributor asked whether to detect touch vs. mouse input and whether changes should apply to all widgets using `TextSelectionControls`.
summaries.119573: Comments show a support side-thread about spell check not working; reporter was redirected to file a separate issue (#158929). No progress on the actual tracking issue.
summaries.80207: Comments from @LongCatIsLooong note that making `text` non-nullable may break builder-style usage and the framework itself sets it to null in two places. A PR was welcomed with the caveat that it might be a breaking change.
summaries.17809: Comments note that `TextEditingController.buildTextSpan` can be overridden to return custom TextSpans (per #30688) as a workaround. A Stack-based workaround (transparent TextField over RichText) was also proposed.
summaries.116456: Comments identify the root cause: the "Dvorak - QWERTY Cmd" layout has a second layer where keys remap to QWERTY when command is held, but Flutter's mapping algorithm does not handle this two-layer behavior, receiving cmd-j instead of cmd-c. No fix proposed.
summaries.150453: Comments clarify API details: `NSTextViewDelegate.textViewWritingToolsWillBegin` is the relevant hook. No PR yet.
summaries.112382: Comments express strong agreement with the proposal; maintainers of packages overriding `TextSelectionGestureDetectorBuilderDelegate` (tightly coupled to `EditableTextState`) also find the current API a maintenance burden. Issue stalled with no plan.
summaries.27918: Comments confirm Bitwarden and 1Password work with some issues. LastPass appears to have its own compatibility problems with virtual views independent of Flutter. A LastPass support case was opened. The issue may be on LastPass's side.
summaries.100456: Only a bot comment about an irrelevant label; the body describes completed work (macOS, GTK, Web, Android done; iOS remaining).
summaries.57591: Comments clarify `showCursor: false` hides the blinking cursor but not the text pointer (drag handle). `readOnly: true` was suggested as an alternative workaround.
summaries.107671: Discussion did not produce a concrete solution; stalled on needing a reduced test case not dependent on custom recognizers.
summaries.27880: Comments note this relates to third-party IME toolbar shortcuts (copy/paste/select) not being supported. Linked to #14047. No resolution.
summaries.145101: One triage comment proposes calling `placeForm()` explicitly when `updateConfig` is received as a fix for the bug.
summaries.22619: Comments clarify the feature is Android-only Java API, all inference runs on-device (5-15ms on Pixel 2), and users cannot opt out.
summaries.179263: Comments note a design challenge: the system context menu can be dismissed by the platform itself, so Flutter's `isVisible` may be stale. Passthrough views may be a prerequisite to give Flutter full ownership of visibility state.
summaries.62397: Comments discuss what new behavior a TextArea widget would add vs. TextField with different defaults. No concrete additions proposed; suggestion to implement as a pub.dev package.
summaries.93724: Comments confirm that setting all `toolbarOptions` to false disables the toolbar but right-click events are still consumed. A `_TransparentTapGestureRecognizer` hack was provided as a workaround.
summaries.106462: Comments identify the crash as occasional and similar to #97789 (also a `KeyboardKeyEmbedderHandler` crash). Suspected trigger is QQ Chinese IME on Windows. Regression introduced between versions 2.5.3 and 2.10.5.
summaries.107130: Comments confirm the limitation remains after 3 years. The only workaround requires implementing `DeltaTextInputClient` directly with significant boilerplate. No public API exposes the current TextInput connection's client state to test tooling.
summaries.54470: Comments clarify an existing animation controller handles button fading but the whole menu needs a size animation. Native iOS 17 uses a size animation for both show/hide and select-all transitions. Show/hide is tracked in #107732; this issue focuses on the select-all size animation.
summaries.92095: Comments confirm the proposal is to add a form-level `onSaved` callback. The async concern is noted as a pre-existing limitation. Treated as a proposal awaiting team input.
summaries.79420: Comments favor moving non-idempotent APIs (like `selectWord`) up to `EditableText` and exposing only idempotent APIs from `RenderEditable`.
summaries.72023: Discussion suggests making `TextButton` support configurable alignment. PR #73894 added the `alignment` parameter but text selection toolbar overflow buttons still need to use it, and the parent layout needs to size to the largest intrinsic width of its children.
summaries.111213: Comments note `TextSelectionDelegate` can be removed once deprecations from PR #107193 expire. A commenter planned to open a PR then unassigned themselves. The issue was incorrectly closed with #107193.
summaries.11738: Comments describe the technical solution: `getOffsetForCaret` needs to accept affinity to return two positions at RTL/LTR boundaries. Long-term, `getOffsetForCaret` may be deprecated in favor of easier cursor-drawing APIs.
summaries.159629: A commenter asked if the reporter would be interested in submitting a documentation PR. No other substantive new information.
summaries.88584: Comments identify the request as frame-by-frame keyboard height callbacks synchronized with the keyboard animation. @blasten suggested thread merging (similar to platform views) as a possible solution. No implementation planned.
summaries.133718: Only a bot comment about an irrelevant label; no substantive new information.
summaries.99198: Comments confirm the issue occurs when scancode is 0 (Windows) or the code is empty/Unidentified (Chromium web). A Chromium bug was filed.
summaries.49047: Only one comment suggesting the user submit a patch themselves; the team is unlikely to prioritize this esoteric feature.
summaries.53456: Comments suggest revisiting in iOS 14 to see if behaviors consolidated. Issue #55809 was mentioned as related. No resolution.
summaries.55302: Only one comment explaining that system-wide custom keyboards require building a dedicated keyboard app, not feasible with Flutter.
summaries.18922: One comment highlights the most impactful missing feature: no way to programmatically clear a validation error, leaving empty fields with stale error messages after the user clears text.
summaries.69127: Comments suggest using `TargetPlatform` to resolve the issue. Bot label comment is noise.
summaries.127054: One comment identifies the root cause: Flutter only sends positions of on-screen characters to the engine; when the field scrolls during the floating cursor gesture, selection rects become incorrect. A fix would require updating `EditableTextState._updateSelectionRects` and measuring performance impact.
summaries.44491: Comments confirm horizontal growth is not supported for `TextField` (unlike `Text`). `TextWidthBasis` was mentioned as a reference for how `Text` handles this.
summaries.139379: Comments note the investigation is still ongoing with no timeline. A customer reports being forced to consider alternatives after 1Password 8 started being affected.
summaries.86682: Multiple comments asking for updates across several versions; no fix as of 3.3.2. The Done button on iOS Chrome does not reliably hide the keyboard. No workaround found.
summaries.98306: Only a bot comment about an irrelevant label; no substantive new information.
summaries.119561: Only a bot comment about an irrelevant label; no substantive new information.
summaries.98280: Comments from @Renzo-Olivares confirm ongoing work to allow programmatically pushing state to the undo stack, which would cover this issue. Assignment was later removed due to no updates.
summaries.180871: One comment suggests deprecating `maybeFind`/`find` and replacing with new methods returning `Action<Intent>`, proposing names like `Actions.of`, `Actions.lookup`, or `Actions.get`.
summaries.119088: Comments confirm the `CharacterActivator` workaround is functional but platform-dependent. A proper fix requires an activator using `KeyEvent` (layout-agnostic) rather than `RawKeyEvent`. Cross-platform portability of such shortcuts was noted as the core problem.
summaries.116790: Comments agree the feature is desirable. A compositional approach via `Tooltip` with an `onTriggered` callback checking `isOverflowing` was proposed, but both the callback return type and the `isOverflowing` property are missing. No PR.
summaries.177952: One comment asks if the feature was already merged; the team confirmed it was not and the issue should stay open.
summaries.68549: Comments discuss whether the cursor rect is currently sent on each keypress outside composing mode; ownership of that implementation was unclear between contributors.
summaries.150452: Comments note using TextKit 2 might provide support automatically. A workaround plugin using native text input (`flutter_native_text_input`) was posted. No official fix.
summaries.159567: Comments suggest a plugin protocol design doc would be needed to expose `FlutterTextInputView` via `inputAccessoryView` to native Swift/Obj-C code.
summaries.173492: One comment suggests `autofillHints` and platform channels for AutoFill, noting this is about system context menu items (UIMenuController), similar to the Share button issue.
summaries.107830: Comments note this is related to #89228. The video was re-uploaded confirming the feature request is specifically about text selection (not just cursor movement) in trackpad mode.
summaries.52049: Comments note the `getExtractedText()` API should be tested for responsible usage. Assignment was removed due to no progress.
summaries.23603: Comments reference the `super_clipboard` package as a working solution for cross-platform rich clipboard on all supported platforms. No framework-level fix.
summaries.156018: Draft engine PR #55568 was filed. A limitation noted in triage: Flutter lacks character-level location information needed for accurate gesture-based selection; the entire character bounding box must be contained in the selection gesture.
summaries.132879: Discussion notes Gmail has a time limit between chord keystrokes while vim does not, suggesting a configurable timeout duration would be needed.
summaries.54279: Comments confirm this is iOS-only and ask if behavior can match Android's existing a11y API approach.
summaries.81172: Comments confirm the design intent: uppercase and lowercase share the same logical key under different shift states. However, a later comment notes the team decided to move forward with adding uppercase convenience statics, reopening the issue.
summaries.96920: One comment provides a DartPad reproduction link. Routed to @LongCatIsLooong.
summaries.87748: Comments warn to watch out for different setters on the aliases before consolidating. A contributor started work then stopped.
summaries.46246: One comment from another developer confirms the same use case (search box with up/down arrow navigation conflicting with TextField cursor movement) and asks about workarounds.
summaries.117221: Comments confirm the issue is still valid; the author self-assigned but had not worked on it for 2.5 years and removed the assignment.
summaries.135952: Only a cc to two Flutter team members; no substantive new information.
summaries.67023: Comments note that `contextMenuBuilder` now allows custom submenus. The native iOS context menu (via `SystemContextMenu`, added in #148265) currently has no customization support including submenus.
summaries.111003: Comments suggest this may be fixed via the Scribe handwriting feature (#115607). A PR reviewing Apple Pencil support in the engine was cited as a reference.
summaries.133011: Discussion suggests changing `debugFocusChanges` to a `DiagnosticLevel` enum or adding a separate `debugFocusChangeDiagnosticLevel` variable for finer-grained control of debug output.
summaries.32285: One comment questions the claim about client IDs and notes that autofillable fields do have a stable autofill ID tied to their Element/State.
summaries.173049: Comments identify the root cause: disabled text fields are internally classified as read-only, and read-only fields use a non-text-input semantics object (also used by SelectableText). Distinguishing disabled from selectable read-only fields is required. No PR.
summaries.182819: Comments confirm root cause: `SelectionOverlay._selectionEndpoints` is computed once and not refreshed on scroll. A contributor raised a PR. Labeled for team review.
summaries.132887: Comments confirm `buildCounter` is ignored when `decoration` is null. The suggestion to move `buildCounter` to `InputDecoration` was noted as reasonable pending code review.
summaries.147377: One comment links to related issue #108258.
summaries.119684: Comments point to PR #116549 discussion for context.
summaries.129655: One comment suggests implementing named constructors as a pub.dev package (potentially via a Dart extension) rather than adding them to the framework.
summaries.119538: Comments confirm the behavior should be platform-wide on iOS (not just for Cupertino widgets). A video demonstration was provided.
summaries.148997: Only a bot comment about an irrelevant label; no substantive new information.
summaries.128388: Comments confirm the feature is needed. @gspencergoog clarified this is separate from his OS-level drag-and-drop work. @Renzo-Olivares was noted as having investigated this. The feature works natively on non-Flutter web on macOS.
summaries.93915: Comments clarify the request is specifically for iOS-style `enablesReturnKeyAutomatically` behavior (auto-enabling the return key when the field is non-empty).
summaries.39949: Comments clarify the request is for a TextField that grows horizontally to fit its content (intrinsic width behavior).
summaries.143903: One comment suggests `minLines` could default to `1` instead of deferring to `maxLines`, but this would be a breaking change requiring a dart fix.
summaries.87963: One comment from @justinmc agrees that imperative methods like `showToolbar` and existing flags like `_shouldShowSelectionToolbar` are both confusing. A PR reference was linked for the TODO in question.
summaries.105677: Comments confirm the proposal is reasonable. The team suggested enabling this by default when `obscureText: true`. A third-party plugin `sensitive_clipboard` was published as a workaround. A related iOS request (#103163) was linked.
summaries.44098: Comments note the workaround is using a Column/Text/TextField combination. No API change proposed.
summaries.76349: Comments note the issue was incorrectly closed (PR #87971 was reverted in #88183) and the TODO remains. Confirmed still reproducible on master.
summaries.133763: Comments confirm XF86_Paste/XF86_Copy keys likely need to be in the keycode database and in the default key bindings for copy/paste intents.
summaries.19978: Comments suggest the `reactive_forms` package as an existing solution for dynamic forms.
summaries.120608: Comments note `nativeSpellCheckServiceDefined` may already be true for Samsung, and PR #40924 was filed to suppress incorrect behavior as a short-term fix.
summaries.125427: Comments confirm the feature (spell check suggestions in keyboard strip rather than context menu) is not implemented; context menu is current behavior.
summaries.100590: One irrelevant comment about image picker rotation; no substantive new information.
summaries.177164: Comments confirm none of the linked issues address applying a custom painter to each text field line. The request remains open.
summaries.69460: Comments note the proposal was labeled for the team. No discussion of implementation or priority.
summaries.67951: Comments add that cursor position relative to the placeholder also differs from native iOS. @GaryQian was mentioned for context on how Skia determines cursor size.
summaries.74482: Comments clarify the issue is not just about filtering digits but also enforcing number format rules (single decimal point, etc.). The StackOverflow workaround was noted as insufficient.
summaries.101973: One bot triage comment; no substantive new information.
summaries.173062: Comments investigate whether adding "secure" to the TalkBack announcement is the expected behavior; native Gmail does not include it. The question was raised whether this requires a PR or is achievable in the test assessment app alone.
summaries.111672: One comment asks whether native iOS exposes any public API for the unit converter to avoid duplicating logic in Flutter.
summaries.146994: PR #146995 was a partial fix with outstanding feedback. A comment confirms a fix has been merged (resolved).
summaries.22149: Comments clarify cmd+k works on physical devices (not just the simulator) to show/hide the virtual keyboard when a Bluetooth keyboard is connected. No implementation plan.
summaries.155948: Draft engine PR #55532 was filed after prerequisite #52943 merged. An assignment was set assuming @justinmc would work on it.
summaries.82104: Comments confirm the validator text alignment issue persists. CC to @victorsanni from triage; a question about whether a simple Align workaround is possible was raised.
summaries.165672: Comments suggest this change might also fix #57609. @huycozy confirmed worth looking into.
summaries.108447: Comments provide a string workaround (`'AUTOFILL_HINT_2FA_APP_OTP'`). Assignment removed due to no progress. Android triage asked about the iOS equivalent hint.
summaries.44172: Comments provide a code sample showing the label spacing issue. No API proposal or workaround beyond the Column/Text/TextField pattern.
summaries.150392: Comments confirm the Translation API is Swift-only, requiring engine-level Swift support tracked in #144791. A note was added to that issue.
summaries.24153: Comments add that emoji input availability on iOS is inconsistent. Multiple follow-up questions with no confirmed resolution.
summaries.141100: Comments confirm the Radius.elliptical workaround exists but is suboptimal. The issue has been open for over 2 years with no progress.
summaries.61803: Comments link to the specific thread in PR #59766 that identified the performance issue. No fix implemented.
summaries.124142: One comment identifies the two strategies (`_buildSubtreeWithComposingRegion` and `_buildSubtreeWithoutComposingRegion`) that could be merged by dropping composing region consideration and using only cursor position.
summaries.125424: One comment suggests using `TextDecoration.combine` to overlay both the red spell-check underline and the composing underline simultaneously.
summaries.68117: One comment notes Flutter has historically been slow to add features not in the Material spec, and `InputDecorator`'s layout is already complex. The request is considered reasonable but stalled.
summaries.46882: One comment notes this is blocked on Android's lack of a frame-by-frame keyboard animation API (see #45076).
summaries.72172: Comments identify the fix: adding `autofillHints: [AutofillHints.telephoneNumber]` causes GBoard to show contact suggestions, because `keyboardType` is inferred from `autofillHints` but not vice versa. The fix is to infer autofill hints from `keyboardType`.
summaries.179591: One comment notes #172930 may be related.
summaries.13468: Comments discuss that the behavior `isDirectional` is meant to control is currently handled by `DefaultTextEditingShortcuts`; the value could drive that logic instead of the platform.
summaries.77076: Only one comment asking for updates; no fix or investigation.
summaries.146123: One comment acknowledges the gap: there is no public API to trigger a `TextInputAction` from a custom keyboard, and the team needs to investigate whether native Android/iOS expose such an API.
summaries.40669: Comments suggest this could be an argument to `save()` instead of a separate method.
summaries.149067: One comment questions whether adding `canRequestFocus` to `TextField` was intentional; no resolution.
summaries.42820: Comments note `RenderEditable` is in the rendering layer without `BuildContext`. The workaround is accepting the platform as a parameter from the caller who has `BuildContext` access.
summaries.91752: Comments confirm iOS can also programmatically change the keyboard language. A reference to issue #99606 (preferred keyboard locale) was added for related tracking.
summaries.116469: Comments from @gspencergoog clarify that `KeyboardListener` does not strictly require a `FocusNode`; it could use `HardwareKeyboard.addHandler` instead. The FocusNode allows participation in the focus tree so descendants can handle events first.
summaries.150909: Comments ask for the source of Shadow usage in TextField (linking to Shadow and BoxShadow class docs). No substantive technical discussion.
